domain: Mauritian sega ravanne (Creole frame drum on thigh): the player's posture state (sitting/crouched/standing) couples to drum skin tension; skin loosens during play and must be re-warmed; ensemble tempo gradually accelerates and is driven by drummer; closed-loop coupling among posture, skin tension, ensemble tempo.
specific_mechanism: Three-way closed-loop coupling: posture state → drum skin tension → ensemble tempo; loose skin must be re-warmed periodically; gradual basin-stable acceleration of tempo follows posture cues.
llm_application: SEGA-RAVANNE state-coupled LR basin schedule: monitor effective gradient-noise scale s_t = ||g||^2 / sigma_g^2 during pretraining. When s_t falls below a stall threshold τ_low (skin loose, optimizer struggling), trigger a SHORT high-LR re-warm SPIKE of duration k steps with magnitude proportional to (τ_low - s_t). When s_t high and loss decreases steadily, allow natural LR decay. The trajectory (LR_t, s_t) forms a basin-stable attractor cycle: spikes recur on stall, decay resumes when s_t restored. Differs from (a) WSD Warmup-Stable-Decay (pre-scheduled stable not state-triggered), (b) cyclic LR (fixed-period cycle not state-triggered), (c) cosine decay (no spike), (d) D2Z linear decay (no state feedback). Recombines (i) gradient-noise-scale signal (McCandlish 2018) + (ii) cyclic warm-restart family (SGDR Loshchilov) + (iii) BASIN-STABLE STATE-TRIGGERED spike condition (new combination).
novelty_claim: McCandlish 2018 measures gradient noise scale. Loshchilov SGDR uses periodic warm restarts. WSD uses pre-scheduled phases. SEGA-RAVANNE recombines (a) GRADIENT-NOISE-SCALE measurement + (b) WARM-RESTART SPIKE + (c) STALL-TRIGGERED (not periodic) spike condition. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
